name: ioxplugin
documentation:
# ioxplugin
ioxplugin is a python package that helps you create plugin code from simple JSON.

Use pip to install:

```bash
pip install ioxplugin

modules.name: ioxplugin.validator
documentation:
Useful validation routines

modules.name: ioxplugin.store_ops
documentation: Adds an entry in  the local store

methods.name: ioxplugin.store_ops.PluginStoreOps.addToStore
documentation:
json = the definition 
email address to get the token
developerUser is the local user name on the development machine
returns a PluginMetaData object in case of success, None otherwise

methods.name: ioxplugin.store_ops.PluginStoreOps.install
documentation:
@username = pg3 username
@password = pg3 password
installs the plugin in the next available slot.
it uses the last store_entry.json for installation.
It checks the store_entry.json file to make sure this 
plugin was not installed before ['slot'] and ['installed']. 
If it was already installed, it will return -1.

methods.name: ioxplugin.store_ops.PluginStoreOps._awaitInstallComplete
documentation: wait for the mqtt tread to complete

methods.name: ioxplugin.store_ops.PluginStoreOps._processCommand
documentation: Send a command to the plugin

methods.name: ioxplugin.store_ops.PluginStoreOps._findByName
documentation:
find a plugin by name. If one is found, return it
otherwise, return None

methods.name: ioxplugin.store_ops.PluginStoreOps._findByUuid
documentation:
find a plugin by uuid. If one is found, return it
otherwise, return None

methods.name: ioxplugin.store_ops.PluginStoreOps._getPlugins
documentation: gets an array of all the plugins for this store

methods.name: ioxplugin.store_ops.PluginStoreOps._getNextFreeSlot
documentation:
Finds the next free slot in which a plugin can be installed
returns -1 if none (> 100)

classes.name: ioxplugin.log.IoXPluginLoggedException
documentation:
Use this class to log exceptions including traceback
log + level + include traceback.
level can only be debug, info, warning, error, criticalsuper.__init__(message)
